𝗦𝗡𝗜𝗣 | To enhance AI coding workflows by enabling visual communication, allowing AI agents to render diagrams and previews and users to annotate and provide feedback directly on these visuals.  🔥 Super Hot!

✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📜 JavaScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To enhance AI coding workflows by enabling visual communication, allowing AI agents to render diagrams and previews and users to annotate and provide feedback directly on these visuals.
📅 Created: Feb 2026 • Updated: May 2026

✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Visual communication layer for AI agents
✅ Capture, annotate, and render diagrams/previews
✅ Integration with AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.)
✅ Menu bar / system tray application with CLI
✅ Supports Mermaid diagrams, HTML previews, and image annotation
✅ Screenshot library with search and organization capabilities
✅ OCR for text extraction from images
✅ MCP server for agents without shell access


✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐ 208 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 18 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 4 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Snip is a visual communication tool for AI agents, enabling users to capture, annotate, and render diagrams and previews directly within their AI coding workflows. It integrates with AI coding assistants like Claude Code to replace textual descriptions with visual outputs, facilitating a more intuitive review and iteration process.

✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/rixinhahaha/snip

𝗥𝗘𝗖𝗟𝗜𝗣 | Downloading videos and audio from a wide variety of websites through a user-friendly web interface.  📈 Growing!

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 💻 HTML 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Downloading videos and audio from a wide variety of websites through a user-friendly web interface.
📅 Created: Mar 2026 • Updated: May 2026

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Download videos from 1000+ supported sites (via yt-dlp)
✅ MP4 video or MP3 audio extraction
✅ Quality/resolution picker
✅ Bulk downloads
✅ Automatic URL deduplication
✅ Clean, responsive UI
✅ Single Python file backend


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 4.5K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 778 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 20 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

ReClip is a self-hosted, open-source media downloader that allows users to download videos and audio from over 1000 websites. It features a clean web UI, supports MP4 and MP3 formats, and offers quality selection and bulk download capabilities.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/averygan/reclip

𝗛𝗢𝗠𝗘-𝗚𝗔𝗟𝗟𝗘𝗥𝗬 | To provide a private, self-hosted, and feature-rich web gallery for users to organize, browse, and discover their personal photo and video collections, with an emphasis on user privacy and mobile accessibility.  🔥 Super Hot!

❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📜 JavaScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To provide a private, self-hosted, and feature-rich web gallery for users to organize, browse, and discover their personal photo and video collections, with an emphasis on user privacy and mobile accessibility.
📅 Created: Dec 2020 • Updated: May 2026

❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Self-hosted
✅ Open-source
✅ Web gallery
✅ Mobile-friendly
✅ Tagging
✅ AI-powered image discovery
✅ AI-powered face discovery
✅ Geo-reverse lookups
✅ EXIF data support
✅ Calculate previews
✅ Support for Linux, macOS, Windows
✅ Docker image available


❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀

🍴 99 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 39 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

HomeGallery is a self-hosted, open-source web gallery designed for personal photo and video collections. It offers features like tagging, mobile-friendliness, and AI-powered image and face discovery to help users browse and rediscover their media archives privately.

❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀

𝗦𝗨𝗠𝗠𝗔𝗥𝗜𝗭𝗘 | To quickly extract the essential information from web pages, documents, and media content through an accessible CLI and browser extension.  🔥 Super Hot!

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📘 TypeScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To quickly extract the essential information from web pages, documents, and media content through an accessible CLI and browser extension.
📅 Created: Dec 2025 • Updated: May 2026

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Chrome Side Panel chat with streaming agent and history
✅ Video slide extraction with OCR and transcript cards for YouTube and video files
✅ Media-aware summarization (auto-detects audio/video vs. page content)
✅ Support for multiple coding CLI backends (Codex, Claude, Gemini, etc.)
✅ Streaming Markdown output with metrics and cache status
✅ CLI support for URLs, files, podcasts, YouTube, audio/video, and PDFs
✅ Transcript-first media processing with fallback transcription services
✅ Support for local, paid, and free summarization models
✅ Multiple output modes including Markdown, JSON, and metrics


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 5.9K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 377 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 2 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Summarize is a versatile tool that provides concise summaries from various sources including URLs, files, and media like YouTube videos. It offers both a command-line interface (CLI) and a Chrome Side Panel extension for convenient access.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/steipete/summarize
